subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand that science is constantly evolving
Recognize the importance of evidence in science
Understand repeatability and accuracy in experiments
Learn the role of collaboration and peer review
Apply scientific thinking to simple real-world problems

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Science
topics_covered: Science as a continuously evolving field
teaching_strategy: Use of visuals/charts showing historical discoveries
student_activities: List examples of scientific discoveries that changed over time
Discuss how new evidence led to changes
Create a small timeline of discoveries

lesson_plan.title: Observation, Evidence, and Hypotheses
topics_covered: Importance of evidence in validating ideas
teaching_strategy: Guided questioning on observation vs prediction

lesson_plan.title: Experiments and Repeatability
teaching_strategy: Discussion on why repeatability is important

lesson_plan.title: Scientific Models and Theories
topics_covered: Use of models to explain phenomena
Examples of scientific models in real life
student_activities: Identify whether a statement is a hypothesis or theory
Draw simple models to explain observations

lesson_plan.title: Collaboration and Peer Review
teaching_strategy: Simulate peer review in small groups
student_activities: Work in pairs or groups to test a small experiment
Review another group’s experiment and provide feedback
Reflect on how collaboration improves results

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Application
topics_covered: Recap of observations, hypotheses, and theories
Application of scientific thinking to daily life
teaching_strategy: Quiz combining conceptual and simple applied questions
student_activities: Relate scientific principles to everyday life

teacher_tip: Encourage students to link observations to everyday examples. Use group activities and guided discussions to strengthen conceptual understanding without complex calculations.
